theory.concept:
SQLite stores dates as text and provides functions to compute and format them — the concepts here (extracting parts, computing differences) transfer to other databases even though exact function names differ.

- strftime() formats a date into any pattern you specify — e.g. extracting just the year or month
- Date arithmetic (finding differences between two dates) uses julianday(), which converts a date into a single comparable number
- Comparing formatted date strings directly (e.g. strftime('%Y-%m', date_col) = '2024-01') is a common and reliable way to filter by year/month
theory.syntax:
SELECT strftime('%Y', date_column) AS year FROM table_name;
SELECT julianday(date_column1) - julianday(date_column2) AS days_diff FROM table_name;

shared_table.name: Orders

questions.prompt: Select the customer and order month (as a two-digit number) for every order.
Select the customers whose orders were placed in January 2024.
Select the customer and the number of days between their order date and '2024-04-01'.
Select the total order amount grouped by order month.
